title: turnera hilaireana var. lanceolata
intro: Turnera hilaireana var. lanceolata, also known as Mexican damiana, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States, Mexico, and Central America, belonging to the Turneraceae family. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Mexican damiana is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of menopause, among others. Mexican damiana is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, as well as being used in traditional remedies and supplements.
